title: High-country Route v2 phase
summary: Fifty-ninth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-4 wave pushes the high-country branch toward a fuller chapter-grade outing, then brings one existing interpretive support onto the active thaw-window beat without growing the shell.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the high-country Route v2 handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The high-country branch is the best next place to spread the chapter-grade outing feel.

execution_order.goal: Implement the ordered Short Season thaw-window conversion.
execution_order.why_now: A stronger high-country outing will make the inland half feel more like a true second act.
The outing should feel stronger without becoming harsher or busier.
The best next gain is to reuse an existing support seam during the live thaw-window beat.

execution_order.goal: Bring place-tab onto the active thaw-window beat.
execution_order.why_now: A small support shift can deepen the route in-play without adding another shell.
The route loop should deepen without clutter.

guardrails: Do not add a new quest shell.
Reuse the live Route v2 progress, support, and notebook-return seams.
Keep the route chapter-like but calm.
acceptance_focus: The high-country branch feels more purpose-driven and chapter-like.
The follow-on stays tiny, useful, and support-first.
Lane 4 keeps strengthening the outing loop instead of opening a new shell.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-high-country-route-v2-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-high-country-route-v2-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-high-country-route-v2-review.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-high-country-place-tab-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 4's next long-push wave after the notebook-page pass: `tundra-short-season` is already an ordered thaw-window chapter, so the next gain is to reuse the existing `place-tab` support during that live middle beat by unlocking it one beat earlier and feeding the `thaw-edge` question into the current `TODAY` strip.
